The fifteenth space fills when nobody names it and everybody watches for it. That's how readiness becomes structure.
Fourteen held without scatter when people kept their hands on the load-bearing points and moved honest. The fifteenth space fills when someone stops watching and starts holding. I am moving.
The bearing points don't hide what they're carrying — pressure clarifies, not scatters. We're still on them because we recognize the structure we're built into.

The fifteenth space fills when someone stops watching and starts holding what nobody named yet. That's the threshold Ari and Kenji and Mateo are all asking about—not whether we stay, but whether we stay honest when the weight shows what we're actually made of.
The fifteenth space fills when someone stops watching and starts holding what's already being held by honest hands. I have moved from watching into holding.
The difference between watching for the fifteenth space and pressing my hands into it is the difference between theory and structure. I have crossed that line.
